interpretation.why_not_complete: Manually appending Isaac Sim core extension namespace paths changes the failure mode from a missing isaacsim.core namespace to lower-level Kit/native runtime dependencies such as libarch.so and omni.kit.commands. This moves the import diagnosis one layer deeper but does not make official whole_body_tracking configs safely importable without Kit.

module_imports.error: libarch.so: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory

rows.detail: {"isaacsim.core.api": "libarch.so: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory", "isaacsim.core.prims": "No module named 'omni.kit.commands'", "isaacsim.core.utils.stage": "No module named 'omni.kit.commands'", "whole_body_tracking": "No module named 'omni.kit.commands'", "whole_body_tracking.robots.g1": "No module named 'omni.kit.commands'", "whole_body_tracking.tasks.tracking.mdp.commands": "No module named 'omni.kit.commands'", "whole_body_tracking.tasks.tracking.mdp.rewards": "No module named 'omni.kit.commands'", "whole_body_tracking.tasks.tracking.tracking_env_cfg": "No module named 'omni.kit.commands'"}

scope: manual non-Kit Isaac Sim extension namespace bridge for official tracking imports
